sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **Adobe Analytics connector** integrates with the Adobe Analytics 2.0 API. It enables Shielva to sync report suites, segments, and calculated metrics from your Adobe Analytics organization into the knowledge base.

This connector uses **OAuth2 client_credentials grant** via Adobe IMS. The access token is acquired automatically and refreshed before expiry.

**Key capabilities:**
- Authenticate via OAuth2 client_credentials (Adobe IMS)
- Sync all report suites for the company
- Sync segments and calculated metrics
- Run custom reports (ranked, trended) via POST /reports
- List dimensions and metrics per report suite
- Stable document IDs via `SHA-256('report_suite:'+rsid)[:16]` for deduplication
- Automatic token refresh (24h TTL, 30s pre-expiry buffer)
- Automatic retry with exponential back-off on transient failures

children.title: Supported Operations
children.content:
| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `install()` | Validates credentials + acquires OAuth2 token; returns HEALTHY/CONNECTED or OFFLINE |
| `health_check()` | Token + report suite list; returns suite count in message |
| `sync()` | Fetches report suites + segments + calculated metrics; normalizes to ConnectorDocuments |
| `list_report_suites()` | GET /reportsuites — all report suites for the company |
| `list_dimensions(rsid)` | GET /dimensions?rsid= — all dimensions for a report suite |
| `list_metrics(rsid)` | GET /metrics?rsid= — all metrics for a report suite |
| `list_segments(rsid)` | GET /segments?rsid= — all segments for a report suite |
| `run_report(rsid, metrics, dimensions)` | POST /reports — run a ranked report |

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
The fastest path to a working Adobe Analytics connector is four steps: install dependencies, configure credentials, instantiate the connector, and call a method.

### 1. Install dependencies

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

### 2. Configure credentials

Collect the values listed in **Configuration** below, then pass them in the `config` dict at construction time:

```python
from connector import AdobeAnalyticsConnector

config = {
    "client_id": "<string>"
    "client_secret": "<password>"
    "company_id": "<string>"
    "organization_id": "<string>"
}

connector = AdobeAnalyticsConnector(
    tenant_id="my-tenant",
    connector_id="adobe_analytics_demo",
    config=config,
)
```

### 3. Initialize the connection

`initialize()` resolves any deferred credentials (e.g. exchanges an OAuth authorization code for an access token), validates them against a low-cost endpoint, and primes the underlying HTTP client.

```python
await connector.initialize()
health = await connector.health_check()
assert health.healthy, "connector did not report healthy"
```

### 4. Call your first method: `list_report_suites`

```python
result = await connector.list_report_suites()
print(result)
```

From here, browse the **API Methods** section for the full list of operations this connector exposes, and **Sync Behavior** to learn how to bulk-ingest Adobe Analytics data into Shielva.

sections.title: Authentication

children.title: Auth Type
children.content: `oauth2` (server-to-server client_credentials grant). Credentials are created in Adobe Developer Console → Projects → OAuth Server-to-Server.

children.title: Install Fields
children.content:
| Field | Key | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| Client ID (API Key) | `client_id` | string | Yes | OAuth2 Client ID from Adobe Developer Console |
| Client Secret | `client_secret` | password | Yes | OAuth2 Client Secret |
| Global Company ID | `company_id` | string | Yes | Short alphanumeric slug used in all API URLs |
| Organization ID | `organization_id` | string | No | IMS Org ID (format: {hex}@AdobeOrg) |

children.title: Token Details
children.content:
| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Token URL | `https://ims-na1.adobelogin.com/ims/token/v3` |
| Grant type | `client_credentials` |
| Scopes | `openid,AdobeID,read_organizations,additional_info.projectedProductContext,additional_info.job_function` |
| TTL | 24 hours (86399s from Adobe) |
| Refresh buffer | 30 seconds before expiry |

Tokens are stored in process memory only — never written to disk or logs.

children.title: Credential Security
children.content: Client secrets are stored AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest by the Shielva vault. Neither `client_secret` nor `access_token` values ever appear in log output. The connector uses read-only API scopes — it cannot modify Adobe Analytics data.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
Every `install_field` listed below is collected from the user **once** at install time and stored in the connector's encrypted config. `config_keys` are values the connector derives at runtime (e.g. tokens) and persists for subsequent calls.

### Install fields

| Key | Label | Type | Required | Description |
|-----|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `client_id` | Client ID (API Key) | `string` | ✅ | Your Adobe Analytics OAuth2 Client ID (API Key). Found in Adobe Developer Console → Projects → Your Project → Credentials. |
| `client_secret` | Client Secret | `password` | ✅ | Your Adobe Analytics OAuth2 Client Secret. Keep this secure — it is never logged. |
| `company_id` | Global Company ID | `string` | ✅ | Your Adobe Analytics Global Company ID. Used in all API URLs: analytics.adobe.io/api/{company_id}/... |
| `organization_id` | Organization ID | `string` | — | Your Adobe IMS Organization ID (optional). Format: {hex}@AdobeOrg. Found in Adobe Admin Console. |

### Runtime config keys

_No runtime-derived config keys for this connector._

### Defaults & validation

- All values are validated on `initialize()` — missing required fields raise `MissingConfigError`, malformed values raise `InvalidConfigError`.
- Numeric and boolean fields are coerced from strings (e.g. `"true"` → `True`) so configuration loaded from environment variables is accepted unchanged.
- Secrets are never echoed back in responses or logs; only field keys appear in audit entries.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All methods are implemented in `connector.py` and delegate HTTP calls to `AdobeAnalyticsHTTPClient` in `client/http_client.py`.

children.title: install()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def install(self) -> InstallResult`

Validates credentials and establishes the connector.

**Steps:**
1. Check `client_id` non-empty → MISSING_CREDENTIALS if not.
2. Check `client_secret` non-empty → MISSING_CREDENTIALS if not.
3. Check `company_id` non-empty → MISSING_CREDENTIALS if not.
4. Call `get_token()` via `with_retry`.
5. On success, create fresh http_client.

**Returns:**
- `InstallResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED, message='Connected to Adobe Analytics')`
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` — missing field
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` — 401/403
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=FAILED)` — network/server error

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def health_check(self) -> HealthCheckResult`

Acquires token and lists report suites to verify connectivity.

**Returns:**
- `HealthCheckResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED, message='Connected to Adobe Analytics (2 report suite(s))')`
- `HealthCheckResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` on 401/403
- `HealthCheckResult(health=DEGRADED, auth_status=FAILED)` on network error

children.title: sync()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def sync(self, full=False, since=None, kb_id='') -> SyncResult`

Full sync across three data categories:
1. **Report suites** — `GET /reportsuites` → all suites normalized to ConnectorDocuments
2. **Segments** — `GET /segments?rsid={first_rsid}` → segments for first report suite
3. **Calculated metrics** — `GET /calculatedmetrics` → all calculated metrics

Segment and calculated metric failures are non-fatal.

**Returns:** `SyncResult` with documents_found, documents_synced, documents_failed.
Status: `COMPLETED` when no failures; `PARTIAL` when some fail or zero docs found.

children.title: list_report_suites()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_report_suites(self) -> list[dict]`

Returns all report suites for the company.

**Returns:** List of report suite dicts with rsid, name, currency, timezoneZoneinfo, status.

children.title: list_dimensions(report_suite_id)
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_dimensions(self, report_suite_id: str) -> list[dict]`

Returns all dimensions for a report suite.

**Parameters:**
- `report_suite_id` — RSID string (e.g. 'mycompany.prod')

**Returns:** List of dimension dicts with id, name, type.

children.title: list_metrics(report_suite_id)
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_metrics(self, report_suite_id: str) -> list[dict]`

Returns all metrics for a report suite.

**Parameters:**
- `report_suite_id` — RSID string

**Returns:** List of metric dicts with id, name, type.

children.title: list_segments(report_suite_id)
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_segments(self, report_suite_id: str) -> list[dict]`

Returns all segments for a report suite.

**Parameters:**
- `report_suite_id` — RSID string

**Returns:** List of segment dicts with id, name, description, owner, tags.

children.title: run_report(report_suite_id, metrics, dimensions, date_range)
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def run_report(self, report_suite_id: str, metrics: list[str], dimensions: list[str], date_range: str = '') -> dict`

Runs a ranked Adobe Analytics report.

**Parameters:**
- `report_suite_id` — RSID string
- `metrics` — list of metric IDs (e.g. `['metrics/visits', 'metrics/pageviews']`)
- `dimensions` — list of dimension IDs (e.g. `['variables/page']`); first one used
- `date_range` — optional ISO-8601 date range string

**Returns:** Raw JSON report response with `rows` and `columns`.

sections.title: Data Model
sections.content: Normalized documents produced by the helper functions in `helpers/utils.py`.

children.title: Report Suite ConnectorDocument
children.content:
```json
{
  "source_id": "<sha256('report_suite:mycompany.prod')[:16]>",
  "title": "Adobe Analytics report suite: Production Site",
  "content": "Report Suite ID: mycompany.prod\nName: Production Site\nCurrency: USD\nTimezone: America/New_York\nStatus: active",
  "source_url": "https://analytics.adobe.com",
  "metadata": {
    "rsid": "mycompany.prod",
    "name": "Production Site",
    "currency": "USD",
    "timezone": "America/New_York",
    "status": "active",
    "type": "report_suite"
  }
}
```

children.title: Segment ConnectorDocument
children.content:
```json
{
  "source_id": "<sha256('segment:s300012345_5f123abc')[:16]>",
  "title": "Adobe Analytics segment: Mobile Users",
  "content": "Segment ID: s300012345_5f123abc\nName: Mobile Users\nDescription: All visits from mobile devices\nOwner: John Doe\nTags: mobile, devices",
  "source_url": "https://analytics.adobe.com",
  "metadata": {
    "id": "s300012345_5f123abc",
    "name": "Mobile Users",
    "description": "All visits from mobile devices",
    "owner": "John Doe",
    "tags": ["mobile", "devices"],
    "type": "segment"
  }
}
```

children.title: Calculated Metric ConnectorDocument
children.content:
```json
{
  "source_id": "<sha256('calculated_metric:cm300012345_5fabc123')[:16]>",
  "title": "Adobe Analytics calculated metric: Bounce Rate",
  "content": "Calculated Metric ID: cm300012345_5fabc123\nName: Bounce Rate\nDescription: Percentage of single-page visits\nFormula: metric('bounces')/metric('entries')\nOwner: Analytics Team\nPolarity: negative\nPrecision: 1",
  "source_url": "https://analytics.adobe.com",
  "metadata": {
    "id": "cm300012345_5fabc123",
    "name": "Bounce Rate",
    "formula": "metric('bounces')/metric('entries')",
    "owner": "Analytics Team",
    "type": "calculated_metric"
  }
}
```

children.title: Document ID Stability
children.content:
Document IDs are stable across syncs:
- Report suite: `SHA-256('report_suite:' + rsid)[:16]`
- Segment: `SHA-256('segment:' + id)[:16]`
- Calculated metric: `SHA-256('calculated_metric:' + id)[:16]`

The same Adobe Analytics data always produces the same Shielva document ID, enabling upsert deduplication without storing seen-ID lists.

sections.title: Sync Behavior
sections.content:
`sync()` is the bulk ingestion entry point. A single call pulls every resource this connector knows how to sync from Adobe Analytics, normalizes each one into a `ConnectorDocument`, and returns a `SyncResult` summarizing what was processed.

### Sync lifecycle

1. **Listing** — the connector requests the first page of each resource type from the provider's list endpoint.
2. **Pagination** — subsequent pages are fetched until the provider signals end-of-stream (cursor exhaustion, `has_more=false`, or empty page).
3. **Normalization** — each raw record is passed through the resource-specific `normalize_*` helper, which extracts fields, computes the `source_id`, and emits a `ConnectorDocument`.
4. **Deduplication** — when the same resource appears across pages (rare, but possible during eventual-consistency windows), the deterministic `source_id` ensures the downstream knowledge base treats it as a single record, not a duplicate.
5. **Partial-failure tolerance** — a per-resource failure is logged and the sync continues with the next resource. The returned `SyncResult` exposes `status=PARTIAL` and an `errors` list when this happens.

### Pagination strategy

- **Cursor-based** providers (most modern APIs) — the connector tracks the `next_cursor` field on each page and stops when the provider omits it.
- **Offset/limit** providers — the connector requests fixed-size pages and stops when a page returns fewer records than `limit`.
- **Token-based (e.g. `next_page_token`)** — same as cursor-based but the token is opaque and forwarded verbatim.

### Rate-limit handling

When the provider returns `429 Too Many Requests`, the connector honors the `Retry-After` header if present, otherwise applies exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s capped at 30s) with full jitter. The retry budget per request is bounded so a misbehaving provider cannot stall the sync indefinitely.

### Incremental sync

The connector treats every `sync()` as a full re-walk of the listing endpoints. Idempotency is achieved via stable `source_id`s rather than incremental cursors. If the provider exposes a `since` / `updated_after` parameter, the connector uses the last successful sync timestamp; otherwise it pulls the full list and lets deduplication handle overlap.

### Features enabled

This connector is configured with: calculated_metrics, dimensions, error_handling, metrics, normalizer, oauth2, rate_limiting, report_suites, reports, retry, segments, token_refresh.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Every failure mode this connector encounters maps to a typed exception. Catching the base class — `AdobeAnalyticsError` — handles every connector-raised error in one block; catching a specific subclass lets you react to a particular failure mode.

### Exception hierarchy

```
AdobeAnalyticsError(Exception)
  AdobeAnalyticsAuthError(AdobeAnalyticsError)
  AdobeAnalyticsNetworkError(AdobeAnalyticsError)
  AdobeAnalyticsNotFoundError(AdobeAnalyticsError)
  AdobeAnalyticsRateLimitError(AdobeAnalyticsError)
```

### When each is raised

- **`AdobeAnalyticsError`** — Base exception for all Adobe Analytics connector errors.
- **`AdobeAnalyticsAuthError`** — Raised when Adobe IMS or the Analytics API rejects credentials (401/403).
- **`AdobeAnalyticsNetworkError`** — Raised on transient network failures (timeouts, connection errors).
- **`AdobeAnalyticsNotFoundError`** — Raised when a requested Adobe Analytics resource does not exist (404).
- **`AdobeAnalyticsRateLimitError`** — Raised on 429 Too Many Requests from Adobe Analytics.

### Retry policy

- **Transient HTTP errors** (network resets, `502`, `503`, `504`) — retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff + jitter.
- **Rate-limit responses** (`429`) — retry honoring `Retry-After`, capped at 5 attempts.
- **Authentication errors** (`401`, `403`) — not retried; surface immediately so the caller can re-authorize.
- **4xx client errors** (`400`, `404`, `422`) — not retried; raised as a typed exception so the caller can fix the request.

### Circuit breaker

A per-connector circuit breaker opens after 5 consecutive failures within a 30-second window and stays open for 30 seconds. While open, every method call raises `CircuitBreakerOpen` immediately instead of incurring the network round-trip — protecting the provider from a thundering-herd retry storm when it is genuinely down.

sections.title: Testing
sections.content:
Every connector ships with a unit test suite under `tests/`. Unit tests mock the HTTP layer so they run offline; integration tests (if configured) hit the live provider API and require valid credentials in the environment.

### Running unit tests

```bash
cd adobe_analytics_connector
python -m pytest -q tests/
```

### Test layout

- `tests/test_connector.py` — the primary suite. One `test_*` function per public connector method, covering the happy path, the empty-result path, and the error path for each.
- `tests/__init__.py` — package marker; no shared fixtures live here.
- HTTP responses are mocked with `httpx.MockTransport` or `pytest-httpx` so the tests are deterministic and fast.

### Test coverage at a glance

This connector ships **100** test functions. The suite covers authentication, pagination, normalization, the connector's main API methods, and the error paths for each one.

### Integration tests

Integration tests are gated by the presence of credentials in the environment (e.g. `RUN_INTEGRATION=1` plus the connector's install-field values prefixed with `ADOBE_ANALYTICS_`). When the gate is absent, the suite short-circuits with a `pytest.skip` so CI doesn't fail in environments without live access.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
The most common failure modes for the Adobe Analytics connector and how to resolve them.

| Symptom | Probable cause | Resolution |
|---------|----------------|------------|
| `401 Unauthorized` on every call | API key revoked, OAuth token expired without a valid refresh token, or wrong account | Re-run the install flow with fresh credentials; check the provider audit log for forced revocation |
| `403 Forbidden` on specific endpoints | Authenticated identity lacks the required scope or role | Grant the missing scope (see **Authentication**), then re-authorize |
| `404 Not Found` for resources that should exist | Tenant / workspace ID mismatch — credentials belong to a different scope | Verify the workspace ID in `install_fields` matches the credential's scope |
| `429 Too Many Requests` storm | Sync running concurrently with another integration that shares the rate-limit bucket | Stagger sync schedules; the connector already retries with backoff but cannot exceed the provider's per-account quota |
| Sync returns `PARTIAL` status | One or more resource types failed (see `SyncResult.errors`) | Re-run sync; if the same resource fails repeatedly, inspect the error in the audit log and open an issue |
| `CircuitBreakerOpen` raised | 5 consecutive failures tripped the breaker | Wait 30s — the breaker self-heals — or call `health_check()` to force a probe |
| Connector loads but `health_check()` reports `unhealthy` | Credentials present but invalid | Inspect `health.error` for the upstream message; usually a typo'd key or revoked grant |
| `MissingConfigError` at startup | Required `install_field` not supplied | Re-install via the UI and fill the highlighted field |

### Diagnostic checklist

1. `await connector.health_check()` — quickest probe; returns the upstream's verbatim error if the credentials are bad.
2. Check the connector audit entries in shielva-sop — every API call is logged with `outcome` (`Success`/`Denied`/`Failure`) and a `correlation_id`.
3. Reproduce the failing call in isolation with `curl` against the provider API using the same credentials — if curl also fails, the credentials are the problem, not the connector.
4. Inspect `connector.metrics` if you have observability enabled — request counts, error rates, and p99 latency per method are exported.

sections.title: FAQ
sections.content:
### Does this connector support multiple accounts of the same provider?

Yes. Each Adobe Analytics account is a separate connector *instance* with its own `connector_id`, install fields, and encrypted credentials. The provider key in the catalog is shared, but the multi-tenant data layer keeps the instances strictly isolated.

### Can I sync only some resource types?

Yes. The connector exposes a `sync_resources` config option (a list of resource type strings). When set, `sync()` walks only the listed types and skips the rest. Leave it unset to sync everything the connector knows about.

### How does deletion propagate?

On each `sync()`, the connector compares the set of `source_id`s returned by Adobe Analytics against the set it last persisted to the knowledge base. IDs that are absent on the new walk are deleted from the knowledge base, so deletions in Adobe Analytics propagate to Shielva on the next sync.

### What happens during a rate-limit hit?

The connector inspects `Retry-After` and waits the indicated interval. If the header is absent, it falls back to exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s) with full jitter, retrying up to 5 times. After that it raises a `RateLimitError` so the caller can decide whether to back off further.

### Are credentials encrypted at rest?

Yes — AES-256-GCM with a per-tenant data encryption key derived via HKDF-SHA256 from the master key. Plaintext exists in memory only for the lifetime of an in-flight request; the secret never appears in logs, audit entries, or telemetry.

### How do I add a new method?

1. Add the method to the `AdobeAnalyticsConnector` class in `connector.py`.
2. Add a corresponding `test_<method>` to `tests/test_connector.py` covering happy/empty/error paths.
3. Re-run the documentation generator — the new method will appear in **API Methods** automatically.

### Can the connector run in a worker / serverless context?

Yes. The connector is fully `async` and holds no global state, so it is safe to instantiate per-request (or per-task) inside Celery, AWS Lambda, or a worker pool. For long-running processes, instantiate once and reuse — the underlying HTTP client maintains a keep-alive connection pool.

sections.title: Changelog
sections.content:
All notable changes to the **Adobe Analytics connector** are recorded here. This project follows [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/): `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` where `MAJOR` is incremented for breaking interface changes, `MINOR` for backwards-compatible features, and `PATCH` for bug fixes.

## [1.0.0] — Current

### Added
- Initial release of the Adobe Analytics connector.
- Full sync of all primary resource types with normalization into `ConnectorDocument`.
- Public connector methods for direct API access (see **API Methods**).
- Typed exception hierarchy with retry-aware classification (see **Error Handling**).
- Health check endpoint for fast credential validation.

### Stability
- Multi-tenant safe — every query is scoped by `tenant_id`.
- Idempotent ingestion — deterministic `source_id` ensures resyncs don't duplicate.
- Circuit breaker protects upstream during sustained outage.

### Upgrade notes
- This is the initial release; no upgrade path is required.
- Future PATCH releases are guaranteed not to break existing config or method signatures.
- MINOR releases may introduce new optional methods or features; existing call sites continue to work unchanged.
- MAJOR releases will be accompanied by a migration guide in this section.
